title: nepeta amethystina subsp. mallophora
intro: Nepeta amethystina subsp. mallophora, also known as Purple Catmint, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae, characterized by its fragrant purple flowers and gray-green foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Purple Catmint include its ability to calm the nervous system, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote a restful sleep. The plant can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, including insomnia, digestive problems, and skin irritations.
